title: Rolling Nonlinear Time-Series Metrics
text: rollNonlinear computes rolling-window nonlinear time-series metrics intraday OHLC data. designed quantitative research workflows need characterise dynamical regime price series — e.g., trend persistence (Hurst), scaling behaviour (DFA), complexity (sample/ approximate entropy), chaotic divergence (Lyapunov, correlation dimension) — time rather single point. Key features: Six built-metrics: hurst, dfa, sample_entropy, approx_entropy, lyapunov, corr_dim. Extensible registry: add custom metrics via register_metric(). Built-series transforms: series_close(), series_log_returns(), series_typical(), series_range(), custom function(dt). Multi-symbol dispatch: accepts data.table symbol column processes symbol independently. Optional parallel execution via mirai multi-symbol workloads.

title: Rolling Nonlinear Time-Series Metrics
text: roll_nonlinear() expects data.table (data.frame, coerced) minimum:

what: 3. Basic usage — single symbol, Hurst + DFA
title: Rolling Nonlinear Time-Series Metrics
text: row corresponds one rolling window. window_start_idx window_end_idx 1-based row indices input symbol’s data.
code: single <- dt[symbol == "AAPL"]  out <- roll_nonlinear(   single,   metrics     = c("hurst", "dfa"),   window_size = 128L,   stride      = 16L ) #> ℹ Rolling 2 metrics across 1 symbol (sequential mode). #> Registered S3 method overwritten by 'quantmod': #>   method            from #>   as.zoo.data.frame zoo  head(out) #> Key: <symbol, time> #>    symbol                time window_start_idx window_end_idx     hurst #>    <char>              <POSc>            <int>          <int>     <num> #> 1:   AAPL 2024-01-02 11:37:00                1            128 0.7679974 #> 2:   AAPL 2024-01-02 11:53:00               17            144 0.7272546 #> 3:   AAPL 2024-01-02 12:09:00               33            160 0.7534161 #> 4:   AAPL 2024-01-02 12:25:00               49            176 0.7979166 #> 5:   AAPL 2024-01-02 12:41:00               65            192 0.6861397 #> 6:   AAPL 2024-01-02 12:57:00               81            208 0.8831254 #>    dfa_alpha #>        <num> #> 1:  1.767997 #> 2:  1.727255 #> 3:  1.753416 #> 4:  1.797917 #> 5:  1.686140 #> 6:  1.883125

title: Rolling Nonlinear Time-Series Metrics
text: Pass full multi-symbol table: result keyed (symbol, time).
code: out_multi <- roll_nonlinear(   dt,   metrics     = c("hurst", "dfa"),   window_size = 128L,   stride      = 32L ) #> ℹ Rolling 2 metrics across 2 symbols (sequential mode).  out_multi[, .(symbol, time, hurst, dfa_alpha)][1:6] #> Key: <symbol, time> #>    symbol                time     hurst dfa_alpha #>    <char>              <POSc>     <num>     <num> #> 1:   AAPL 2024-01-02 11:37:00 0.7679974  1.767997 #> 2:   AAPL 2024-01-02 12:09:00 0.7534161  1.753416 #> 3:   AAPL 2024-01-02 12:41:00 0.6861397  1.686140 #> 4:   AAPL 2024-01-02 13:13:00 0.4350000  1.435000 #> 5:   AAPL 2024-01-02 13:45:00 0.3696960  1.369696 #> 6:   AAPL 2024-01-02 14:17:00 0.4732562  1.473256

title: Rolling Nonlinear Time-Series Metrics
code: lr_out <- roll_nonlinear(   dt[symbol == "AAPL"],   series      = series_log_returns(),   metrics     = "hurst",   window_size = 128L,   stride      = 32L ) #> ℹ Rolling 1 metric across 1 symbol (sequential mode). #> Warning: Metric "hurst" failed (window [1, 128]); filling `NA`. #> ✖ Window contains NA values. head(lr_out[, .(time, hurst)]) #>                   time     hurst #>                 <POSc>     <num> #> 1: 2024-01-02 11:37:00        NA #> 2: 2024-01-02 12:09:00 0.5111897 #> 3: 2024-01-02 12:41:00 0.4844938 #> 4: 2024-01-02 13:13:00 0.6716737 #> 5: 2024-01-02 13:45:00 0.7281123 #> 6: 2024-01-02 14:17:00 0.7754734

title: Rolling Nonlinear Time-Series Metrics
text: function function(dt) -> numeric accepted:
code: log_close <- function(dt_chunk) log(dt_chunk[["close"]])  out_logclose <- roll_nonlinear(   dt[symbol == "AAPL"],   series      = log_close,   metrics     = "hurst",   window_size = 128L,   stride      = 32L ) #> ℹ Rolling 1 metric across 1 symbol (sequential mode). head(out_logclose[, .(time, hurst)]) #>                   time     hurst #>                 <POSc>     <num> #> 1: 2024-01-02 11:37:00 0.7794158 #> 2: 2024-01-02 12:09:00 0.7579486 #> 3: 2024-01-02 12:41:00 0.6929557 #> 4: 2024-01-02 13:13:00 0.4284412 #> 5: 2024-01-02 13:45:00 0.3694222 #> 6: 2024-01-02 14:17:00 0.4664336

title: Rolling Nonlinear Time-Series Metrics
text: Use metric_args override per-metric defaults: Note: r_frac scales tolerance r r_frac * sd(x) per window (volatility-normalised). volatility-sensitive analysis, pass r explicitly instead.
code: ent_out <- roll_nonlinear(   dt[symbol == "AAPL"],   metrics     = c("sample_entropy", "approx_entropy"),   window_size = 100L,   stride      = 20L,   metric_args = list(     sample_entropy = list(dimension = 2L, r_frac = 0.15),     approx_entropy = list(dimension = 2L, r_frac = 0.20)   ) ) #> ℹ Rolling 2 metrics across 1 symbol (sequential mode). head(ent_out[, .(time, sample_entropy, approx_entropy)]) #>                   time sample_entropy approx_entropy #>                 <POSc>          <num>          <num> #> 1: 2024-01-02 11:09:00      0.6733446      0.4702413 #> 2: 2024-01-02 11:29:00      0.5453863      0.3573229 #> 3: 2024-01-02 11:49:00      0.4759168      0.3488843 #> 4: 2024-01-02 12:09:00      0.5796090      0.4255343 #> 5: 2024-01-02 12:29:00      0.6505876      0.4544956 #> 6: 2024-01-02 12:49:00      0.4568871      0.3062640

title: Rolling Nonlinear Time-Series Metrics
text: workloads many symbols, parallel = TRUE dispatches symbol local mirai daemon:
code: # One-time setup (optional — rollNonlinear manages daemons automatically) rn_daemons(4L)  out_par <- roll_nonlinear(   dt,   metrics     = c("hurst", "dfa"),   window_size = 128L,   stride      = 32L,   parallel    = TRUE )  rn_stop_daemons()

title: Rolling Nonlinear Time-Series Metrics
text: Use n_workers override automatic core count:
code: roll_nonlinear(dt, parallel = TRUE, n_workers = 2L)

title: Rolling Nonlinear Time-Series Metrics
text: Parallel execution setup overhead (~100–500 ms). Rule thumb: Worth : ≥ 10 symbols ≥ 1 000 bars . worth : small datasets (< ~10 symbols < ~1 000 bars ) — sequential usually faster.

title: Rolling Nonlinear Time-Series Metrics
text: Register custom metric returns mean absolute deviation: Registered metrics work identically sequential parallel execution.
code: register_metric(   name    = "mad_metric",   fn      = function(x, .args = list()) {     c(mad_metric = mean(abs(x - mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)), na.rm = TRUE))   },   outputs = "mad_metric" )  available_metrics() #> [1] "approx_entropy" "corr_dim"       "dfa"            "hurst"          #> [5] "lyapunov"       "mad_metric"     "sample_entropy"  mad_out <- roll_nonlinear(   dt[symbol == "AAPL"],   metrics     = "mad_metric",   window_size = 64L,   stride      = 16L ) #> ℹ Rolling 1 metric across 1 symbol (sequential mode). head(mad_out[, .(time, mad_metric)]) #>                   time mad_metric #>                 <POSc>      <num> #> 1: 2024-01-02 10:33:00   3.006953 #> 2: 2024-01-02 10:49:00   1.809379 #> 3: 2024-01-02 11:05:00   3.966879 #> 4: 2024-01-02 11:21:00   7.093376 #> 5: 2024-01-02 11:37:00   6.786955 #> 6: 2024-01-02 11:53:00   3.537289

title: Rolling Nonlinear Time-Series Metrics
text: Lyapunov & correlation dimension require substantial data (≥ 500–1 000 bars per window) produce meaningful estimates. Short windows yield unreliable values; use \donttest{} guards scripts. Sample/approximate entropy r_frac volatility-normalised default. Pass r explicitly need volatility-sensitive comparisons. Parallel overhead: small datasets, sequential mode faster. Prefer parallel = TRUE wide, large-bar symbol sets.

title: List available metrics — available_metrics
text: Returns names metrics registered metric registry, including built-user-registered metrics.

title: List available metrics — available_metrics
code: available_metrics()

title: List available metrics — available_metrics
text: sorted character vector metric names.

title: List available metrics — available_metrics
code: available_metrics() #> [1] "approx_entropy" "corr_dim"       "dfa"            "hurst"          #> [5] "lyapunov"       "sample_entropy"

what: Register a custom rolling metric — register_metric
title: Register a custom rolling metric — register_metric
text: Adds new metric (replaces existing one) package metric registry. registered, metric name available use roll_nonlinear_one() roll_nonlinear().

title: Register a custom rolling metric — register_metric
code: register_metric(name, fn, outputs, defaults = list(), min_len = NULL)

title: Register a custom rolling metric — register_metric
text: name single string. metric name (key registry). fn function formals (x, .args = list()) accepts numeric vector x list named arguments .args, returns named numeric vector names matching outputs. outputs Non-empty, unique character vector output column names fn returns. defaults Named list default arguments passed fn .args. User-supplied metric_args override via utils::modifyList(). min_len function function(args) -> integer returns advisory minimum window size given resolved args. NULL, defaults function() 2L.

title: Register a custom rolling metric — register_metric
text: Invisibly NULL. Called side effects.

title: Register a custom rolling metric — register_metric
code: my_fn <- function(x, .args = list()) c(my_mean = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)) register_metric("my_mean", my_fn, outputs = "my_mean") available_metrics() #> [1] "approx_entropy" "corr_dim"       "dfa"            "hurst"          #> [5] "lyapunov"       "my_mean"        "sample_entropy" # cleanup rm("my_mean", envir = rollNonlinear:::.metric_registry)

what: Query or set mirai daemons — rn_daemons
title: Query or set mirai daemons — rn_daemons
text: Convenience wrapper around mirai::daemons() mirai::status(). arguments, returns current daemon status. integer argument, sets number daemons returns n invisibly.

title: Query or set mirai daemons — rn_daemons
code: rn_daemons(n = NULL)

title: Query or set mirai daemons — rn_daemons
text: n NULL (default) query status, positive integer set number local daemons.

title: Query or set mirai daemons — rn_daemons
text: n = NULL, list returned mirai::status(). Otherwise, n (invisibly).

title: Query or set mirai daemons — rn_daemons
text: Query set mirai daemons

title: Query or set mirai daemons — rn_daemons
code: # \donttest{ if (requireNamespace("mirai", quietly = TRUE)) {   rn_daemons(2L)   # start 2 daemons   rn_daemons()     # query status   rn_stop_daemons() } # }

title: Stop mirai daemons — rn_stop_daemons
text: Calls mirai::daemons(0L) stop currently running local daemons. Safe call even daemons running.

title: Stop mirai daemons — rn_stop_daemons
code: rn_stop_daemons()

title: Stop mirai daemons — rn_stop_daemons
text: NULL invisibly.

title: Stop mirai daemons — rn_stop_daemons
text: Stop mirai daemons

title: Stop mirai daemons — rn_stop_daemons
code: # \donttest{ if (requireNamespace("mirai", quietly = TRUE)) {   rn_daemons(2L)   rn_stop_daemons() } # }

what: rollNonlinear: Rolling Nonlinear Time-Series Metrics Engine — rollNonlinear-package
title: rollNonlinear: Rolling Nonlinear Time-Series Metrics Engine — rollNonlinear-package
text: Computes rolling window nonlinear time-series metrics (Hurst exponent, Detrended Fluctuation Analysis, sample approximate entropy, Lyapunov exponent, correlation dimension) one symbols OHLC-style data.tables. Provides extensible metric registry via register_metric(), built-series transforms, optional parallel execution via mirai multi-symbol workloads.

title: rollNonlinear: Rolling Nonlinear Time-Series Metrics Engine — rollNonlinear-package
text: Maintainer: rollNonlinear Authors rollnonlinear@example.com

what: Rolling nonlinear metrics for multiple symbols — roll_nonlinear
title: Rolling nonlinear metrics for multiple symbols — roll_nonlinear
text: Computes rolling window nonlinear time-series metrics one symbols. Splits input data symbol_col, resolves series via resolve_series(), calls roll_nonlinear_one() chunk sequentially.

title: Rolling nonlinear metrics for multiple symbols — roll_nonlinear
code: roll_nonlinear(   data,   metrics = c("hurst", "dfa"),   window_size = 100L,   stride = 1L,   series = "close",   symbol_col = "symbol",   time_col = "time",   min_obs = window_size,   metric_args = list(),   on_error = c("warn_na", "stop", "silent_na"),   parallel = FALSE,   n_workers = NULL )

title: Rolling nonlinear metrics for multiple symbols — roll_nonlinear
text: data data.table (data.frame, coerced). Must contain least symbol_col, time_col, column(s) implied series. metrics Character vector metric names. Must subset available_metrics(). Default: c("hurst", "dfa"). window_size Positive integer. Number observations per window. Default: 100L. stride Positive integer. Step window anchors. Default: 1L. series Either string (column name) function function(dt) -> numeric returning series analyse. Built-constructors: series_close(), series_log_returns(), series_typical(), series_range(). Default: "close". symbol_col String. Name symbol grouping column. Default: "symbol". time_col String. Name time column. Must strictly increasing within symbol. Default: "time". min_obs Positive integer (<= window_size). Minimum observations required per window. Default: window_size. metric_args Named list per-metric argument overrides. Names must subset metrics. Default: list(). on_error Error-handling policy passed roll_nonlinear_one(). One "warn_na" (default), "stop", "silent_na". parallel Logical. TRUE one symbol present, execution dispatched local mirai daemons. mirai available, falls back sequential warning. Default: FALSE. n_workers NULL (default) positive integer. Number local mirai daemons start none already running. NULL uses parallelly::availableCores(omit = 1L). Ignored parallel = FALSE daemons already running.

title: Rolling nonlinear metrics for multiple symbols — roll_nonlinear
text: data.table keyed (symbol_col, time_col) one row per valid rolling window per symbol columns: <symbol_col> Symbol identifier. time Anchor time (times[end]). window_start_idx Integer. Window start index. window_end_idx Integer. Window end index. <metric columns> One numeric columns per metric.

title: Rolling nonlinear metrics for multiple symbols — roll_nonlinear

what: Rolling nonlinear metrics for a single symbol — roll_nonlinear_one
title: Rolling nonlinear metrics for a single symbol — roll_nonlinear_one
text: Computes rolling window nonlinear time-series metrics single price (numeric) series using sequential execution.

title: Rolling nonlinear metrics for a single symbol — roll_nonlinear_one
code: roll_nonlinear_one(   x,   times,   metrics = c("hurst", "dfa"),   window_size = 100L,   stride = 1L,   min_obs = window_size,   on_error = c("warn_na", "stop", "silent_na"),   metric_args = list() )

title: Rolling nonlinear metrics for a single symbol — roll_nonlinear_one
text: x Numeric vector. time series values (NAs allowed series; handling per-window based on_error). times strictly-increasing vector timestamps corresponding element x. Must POSIXct, Date, integer. metrics Character vector metric names compute. Must subset available_metrics(). Defaults available metrics. window_size Positive integer. Number observations per rolling window. Default: 100L. stride Positive integer. Step consecutive window anchors. Default: 1L. min_obs Positive integer (<= window_size). Minimum observations required window computed. Default: window_size (fully formed windows ). on_error Error-handling policy metric computation failures (including windows containing NAs): "warn_na" Emit deduplicated cli::cli_warn() fill NA_real_ (default). "stop" Re-throw via cli::cli_abort() including window indices. "silent_na" Fill NA_real_ silently. metric_args Named list per-metric argument overrides, keyed metric name. element passed .args metric wrapper (merged metric's registered defaults via utils::modifyList()). Names must subset metrics. Default: list().

title: Rolling nonlinear metrics for a single symbol — roll_nonlinear_one
text: keyed data.table (keyed time) one row per valid rolling window following columns: time Anchor time (times[end]). window_start_idx Integer. 1-based start index window. window_end_idx Integer. 1-based end index (anchor). hurst Numeric. Hurst exponent ("hurst" metrics). dfa_alpha Numeric. DFA scaling exponent ("dfa" metrics). schema stable even metric values NA. Returns 0-row data.table schema valid windows.

title: Rolling nonlinear metrics for a single symbol — roll_nonlinear_one

text: function function(dt) -> numeric length nrow(dt).

title: Typical price: (High + Low + Close) / 3 — series_typical
code: dt <- data.table::data.table(high = c(10, 20), low = c(8, 18), close = c(9, 19)) fn <- series_typical() fn(dt) #> [1]  9 19

title: rollNonlinear 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Rolling nonlinear metrics (Hurst, DFA, sample/approximate entropy, Lyapunov, correlation dimension) intraday OHLC data. Multi-symbol input optional parallel execution via mirai. Extensible metric registry via register_metric(). Built-series transforms: series_close(), series_log_returns(), series_typical(), series_range(). Daemon lifecycle helpers: rn_daemons(), rn_stop_daemons().
